candidates.stmt:
Hello. I’m Gordon Howe, your second Green candidate for Adel and Wharfedeale.

I’m a 71-year old retired postman of 34 years, having formerly worked at Ireland Wood delivery office.

Born in South East London to a working class family, I have been a lifelong trade union activist and a member of the Green Party for many years.

I moved to the Ireland Wood area of Leeds 26 years ago and have lived there ever since.

I’ve entered local politics as I believe we need more representation for ordinary working people.

I am a great believer in community and influence by local people over decisions that affect their neighbourhood.

Hello, I’m Dominic, a 24 year old graduate carbon analyst and your Green Party candidate for Adel and Wharfedale.

I have lived in the ward almost my entire life and am proud to call it home. With two aging grandparent also living in the ward I understand the importance of community and looking out for your neighbours.

In this spirit I have been involved in Bramhope in Bloom, hosting stalls, organising and of course countless hours of gardening! Last year I was on the front desk taking in everyone’s ticket at the Bramhope show – before we got heavily rained on!

Though being a beautiful area to live in, Adel and Wharfedale is not without it’s problems. Through working at the co-op in Adel I had a first-hand experience of shoplifting and physical assault, which I later fed back to the local police in a PACT meeting held in Holt Park.

Also, like many other young people here I am being faced with having to move away from the ward because of the reduced X84 services making my commute and any other travel more difficult.

Building hundreds of new houses whilst decreasing investment in our public transport is obscene and I would jump through any hoop to stop a repeat of this!

Though incredibly grateful for the years of service our councillors have given us, I think it’s time for change and I would be feeling privileged to have your support.

Hi. I’m Catherine, your Green Party candidate for Alwoodley.

I have been a keen climate activist for many years, supporting a wide range of local and national groups and events.

I am a steering group member of Alwoodley 2030 Climate Action Group and have assisted to grow and develop the group over the past 3 years. I have lead on many local projects including trees for schools, the 100 fruit trees project and initiating a local community energy group.

I enjoy nature creation and woodland management with Friends of Gair Woods and Friends of Adel Woods, and have recently joined the Citizens Science water testing group. I’m also a scout leader and am very keen to develop youth interfaith work.

I joined the Green Party because their commitment to social and environmental justice aligns with my values. I realised that to have a bigger impact on the future of our climate and communities, I needed to move from activism to politics.

I’m really excited to take on this role to increase the Green presence and conversations within Alwoodley and I’m looking forward to a Green Future!

A local candidate  for local residents.

May 7th spells the end for the Uniparty.

As your Reform UK  candidate for Ardsley & Robin Hood I will work hard for each and every one of you. Ardsley and Robin Hood will finally have a voice that will be heard.

Hi! I’m Noah Bentley and I’m standing to be your councillor in the ward of Ardsley and Robin Hood.

I am a proud Yorkshireman and have lived around Leeds all my life, so I hold a real passion for the city and surrounding area. I joined the Green Party in September of 2024 after realising that they aligned with my Eco-Socialist values better than any other party could.

Since joining, I have been involved in many different campaigns across the city, from taking part in marches and litterpicks to leafleting and doorknocking sessions.

I have also been working as the social secretary for the Leeds University Green Party Society, helping cultivate a sense of community among young people, and hopefully inspiring them to become activists who can help us to win elections across Leeds.

I am standing because I believe local people deserve a stronger voice and a fairer, greener future. I want to work with residents to improve public services, protect our green spaces, and tackle the cost of living. Together, we can build a community that is more sustainable, inclusive, and responsive to everyone’s needs.

For more than 23 years Armley has been my home. It’s where I’ve built my life, my career and friendships that feel like family.

I truly believe in our community – Armley is full of pride, creativity and determination. It’s a place where people look out for each other and where community spirit runs deep.

For many years our Green councillor Lou Cunningham has worked incredibly hard for Armley – standing up for residents, tackling local issues and helping make our neighbourhood cleaner, greener and fairer.

Now we have the chance to build on that progress.

By electing a second Green councillor, Armley can have an even stronger voice at the council – with Lou and I working together year-round to listen to residents, solve problems and stand up for what matters locally.

We have a lot to be proud of, but there are things that need to change and improve, too.

That’s why I’m standing as the Green Party candidate for Armley – for a cleaner, greener, fairer Armley, a place that everyone feels welcome and that people take pride in.

Connections, Creativity, Commitment

Bringing people together has always been at the heart of what I do. Through creative projects, community events and local initiatives, I’ve spent many years helping connect people and celebrate the spirit of Armley.

My commitment to Armley is practical and hands-on. I co-founded a women’s group to help tackle isolation, I’ve supported the work of Armley Helping Hands with older residents, and regularly attend local meetings to understand what matters most to people living here.

Sharing stories, raising voices, creating change

Alongside this community involvement, I’ve spent more than 30 years working in communications, media, filmmaking and photography – including presenting on BBC radio and local TV, creating and delivering health and care campaigns, and work with many charities locally, regionally and nationally.

Throughout my career I’ve focused on helping people share their stories and amplifying v

I've lived, volunteered, and worked hard in our Beeston and Holbeck communities for many years now. In that time, I've seen the issues we face first-hand: waste & fly-tipping, anti-social behaviour, housing, parking problems, and more. But I've also seen a community of neighbours, of love, with so much potential to thrive.

**Our community is hungry for change**. Myself and your local Green team have been here for you all year-round, taking action on these issues and, importantly, listening to you. I've been a dedicated campaigner for many years now, having co-led successful campaigns on saving libraries, organised practical action, engaged with you at forums/listening events, co-ran community events (including the LS11 Yard Sale), and spoken out on the big issues: planning battles, housing, environmental issues, and more.

On 7th May, a vote for me is a vote to put our community first: **I will be Beeston and Holbeck's voice on Leeds City Council, not the council's voice in our area**. With your support, myself and your Green team can do so much more and bring fresh thinking to the table.

In the last four elections (including the General Election), Greens have come a solid second in our area. And the Greens are surging, in Leeds and across the country, with an entirely different political dynamic and landscape. **I can win here**, but only with your support.

**It's time for change**. For year-round hard-work, for a fresh voice: **VOTE MATT ROGAN, GREEN PARTY, ON MAY 7TH.**

I was born in Cottingley and raised in Beeston, where I live with my partner and our children.

I spend a lot of my time volunteering and listening to people through my roles at Beeston Forum, Beeston Festival, and as a school governor. With a background in care, including kinship care, I got into politics because I’m determined to make our area better for our children and for all our neighbours.

I’ve already worked hard alongside residents to fight service cuts, improve social housing, campaign to save our much-loved local lollipop lady, and challenge the Council’s use of illegal children’s homes. Day in, day out, I also help fix the everyday problems that matter — getting potholes filled, paths resurfaced, missed bins collected, and housing issues sorted.

In 2022, Leeds City Council welcomed its first SDP councillor. Since then, the SDP has taken more seats from Labour in South Leeds than any other party. I joined the SDP because I want to build on that success with you and continue putting local residents first.

If you want a dedicated, hard-working local councillor with a proven track record of standing up for our community and improving it, please vote for me.

candidates.p: The Official Monster Raving Loony Party

candidates.stmt:
Dear neighbours and friends,

I am writing to you as both your councillor and as a candidate in the upcoming election on Thursday 7 May.

Over the past four years it has been the honour of my life to be able to represent my home ward of Burmantofts and Richmond Hill; to serve my neighbours and my community; and to stand up and fight on behalf of residents across Leeds 9.

As your councillor, I have worked tirelessly to be a champion for our area and to help improve our communities. This has included:

- securing funding for playgrounds, outdoor activities and community sports teams and social clubs across the ward, ensuring they can keep on with the vital work they do across our community
- coordinating with the police and partner agencies to help tackle antisocial behaviour and crime, and overseeing the installation of new CCTV equipment locally
- supporting efforts to address environmental crime, fly tipping and littering - attending dozens of litter picks and community clear ups over the years, and reporting those responsible for fly tipping
- pushing for more resources for our communities to address social challenges across Burmantofts and Richmond Hill and to ensure we have a vibrant and thriving local area
- helping hundreds of residents each year with individual items of casework.

Over the last four years I have always done everything within my power to make a difference and see a positive change across our communities. However, there is still more work to do, and my commitment to improving the area we all call home is even stronger than it was when I was first elected.

My priorities going forward will be your priorities: taking on the fly tippers and keeping our streets clean, working with the police to stop crime and antisocial behaviour, improving our parks and green spaces, ensuring the roads and pavements are in good condition, and keeping our local organisations thriving.

If re-elected on Thursday 7 May, I promise that I will continue to give our com

Burmantofts and Richmond Hill is a very diverse and dynamic place, full of amazing people and families. They deserve better!

Many people across the ward are facing growing concerns with the cost of living crisis, which perpetuates health and wellbeing inequalities. Everyone deserves to live and work in a safe and secure economic environment. I’ll be working hard with my Green colleagues to make life easier, not more difficult for everyone in Burmantofts and Richmond Hill.

I’ll be working to bring our amazingly diverse communities together and to create a community spirit which elevates our sense of belonging and makes Burmantofts & Richmond Hill safer for everyone, especially women and children.

Just like many of our Green councillors and activists, myself and my team will be working all year round to keep our streets clean from litter and fly-tipping, from drugs and anti-social behaviour, and from unsafe roads and potholes.

For a brighter future, a Green future, vote Green on 7th May!

I’m Zoë Metcalfe and I am delighted to be the Conservative Party Candidate for the North Yorkshire Police Fire and Crime Commissioner Election. Policing needs to be under great scrutiny, especially after recent tragic events involving women’s safety. Despite the huge challenges the Commissioner faces, I want to improve trust in policing, while supporting the fantastic work of the police and fire service.

As a councillor for Harrogate Borough Council and North Yorkshire County Council, I am experienced in representing residents’ concerns and interests. I pride myself on achieving positive results by working together with a wide array of people and organisations. I also work as a project manager for a property business. The skills and experience I have gained from local government and business will stand me in good stead for this important position I am contesting.

My priorities for the Police and Fire Service are:

 - Urgent action on women and girl’s safety in our community – hold our services to account and implement resources and initiatives to make our society here in North Yorkshire and York a safer place.

 - Tackle anti-social behaviour, burglary, rural crime, pet theft and road safety.

 - Relentless disruption of serious and organised crime – drugs and county lines, serious violence, child sexual exploitation and abuse.

 - Support victims and safeguard the vulnerable.

 - Maximise resources and funding by blue light collaboration to deliver better value for money for taxpayers.

Although there has been significant progress and improvement within both the Police and Fire and Rescue Service, there is still much to deliver.  For a strong and effective Police and Fire Crime Commissioner who will get things done, vote for Zoë Metcalfe.

I’ve loved living in our community for 11 years and I want to help make it a place where everyone can flourish just as my family and I have. I’m honoured to be a candidate in the local elections in May and it’s been a privilege to speak to so many residents about what matters to them during my campaign.

I’m passionate about road safety, protecting and investing in our green spaces and supporting residents who may be struggling with the cost of living. As a mum and an educator I also care deeply about developing spaces where young people can play, create and build skills.

If elected as your councillor my priorities will be

**Protecting and investing in green spaces** – safeguarding our parks, paths, canal and green belt for future generations.

**Improving road safety** – making our roads safer for pedestrians, cyclists and drivers.

**Creating opportunities for young people** – expanding opportunities and support to give every young person the best possible start in life.

**Supporting residents with cost of living** – standing up for families and pensioners facing rising bills and financial pressure and fighting for investment in public services such as libraries, clean streets and improved transport links.

I really love our community and I want to make it a great place to grow up, work and live. I am not a career politician and I can bring my experience of leadership in my other jobs to represent you with focus and professionalism.

**I want to be your councillor because I have a passion for making the place we call home even better, across all our areas of Calverley, Farsley, Pudsey, Rodley, Stanningley, Thornbury and Woodhall. I’d be grateful for your support.**

Ellen has spent a total of 14 years living in Leeds and now enjoys living in Farsley. For the last few years Ellen has worked for charities supporting older people and is also passionate about the environment, women’s issues and LGBTIQA+ rights.

Are you sick of a failing Labour administration?

I am too. I think it’s time to take action as a community to protect people and the planet.

Greens represent the growing number of people taking action into our own hands.

I’m an active campaigner in Leeds, trade unionist and eco-socialist. I believe when we come together, we win.

I have a burning desire to help people. This year I’ve spoken to hundreds of people and they agree that we’ve all been let down by Labour.

It’s time for change.

I’m focused on protecting our communities, standing up for local values, and showing that politics can be done with people, not just to them.

Let’s make politics work for all of us in Chapel Allerton!

I’ve lived in East Leeds since I made it my home in 1991 
I’ve brought up 2 sons here , both veterans, and 2 grandchildren.

I am invested in my local community.  I volunteer with a local organisation, to support our elderly members of the community, as a driver. In addition I am also a volunteer befriender to help reduce loneliness and isolation.

I want to bring East Leeds veterans together to form a union, I think it’s extremely important to support those who have served our country. 
Can you help me?

I’m passionate about girls and women’s safety. 
I’m communicating with the Transport select committee and Leeds City Council to try and make CCTV mandatory in all mini cabs and taxis in Leeds. They have them in Rotherham, why can’t we?

I also want the migrant hotel closed in our area and don’t want HMO’s being imposed on us. 
I will do everything I can to prevent this.

If you elect me, I’ll speak for you

I’ve watched politics from the sidelines for years, shaking my head.

I’m putting my head above the parapet as a normal person who’s worked in industry for years.

I’m not a polititician, ours have failed us for years and continue to do so. 
I’m a mother, a grandmother, a resident and a council taxpayer.

I will be vocal, and will always have the residents of Crossgates & Whinmoor first and foremost in everything I do 
I hope to serve you as your elected representative.

Labour have ignored our community for too long. We need to be proactive rather than react to events. Concerns have been raised, especially after anti-social behaviour incidents in our area and recent armed robberies at Skelton Woods, Stanks and Wellington Hill. I will fight for local people, not play party politics. I am proud to be your local council candidate for our area on Thursday 5th may. I want to use my business and council experience to help and support local residents. Your vote counts. 139 votes separated Labour and Conservatives in 2021!

I have been active in politics for a long time. After twelve years as a Labour Leeds City Councillor I left and joined the Greens 14 years ago. The prime factor in the decision was PFI, although there were many other things I could not defend. Since joining the Green Party I have been active at local, regional and national levels, and have been a candidate in city, parliamentary and European elections on several occasions. I was active in the Remain campaign, leafletting and speaking at public meetings and hustings.

I have lived in the constituency for over thirty years, and have been involved in many voluntary and community activities in the ward from Headingley near the centre of Leeds to the small market town of Otley. My opponent is the disappointing Liberal Democrat MP Greg Mulholland - who did not vote on the Article 50 decision, and has shown an unwillingness to commit on other issues such as Trident.

Who is Mark your local independent candidate?

I am a local resident who has grown up, worked, and lived in the ward all my life. I grew up in Swarcliffe, like many I worked from an early age, delivery newspapers and glass collecting in Swarcliffe Club, etc. Long before mobile phones and the internet, I met my wife in the old Staging Post and went on to live in Whinmoor before moving into Crossgates. I have worked locally as a postman,  construction roles in the area, and now working in health and safety management, also spending time in the armed forces as did my brother, sister and father.

I am not someone who goes home to another area after work or comes back into the area for a photo opportunity. I am a passionate candidate who sees and hears the local problems daily. I have stood before for these reasons, in the previous local elections gaining support and improving my results from those locals fed up with big party system and false promises. I would never consider standing in other areas, unlike our other candidates who frequently do.

As a local resident my family, daughters and friends all live the area.  As a father of 3 daughters, I have had concerns on issues such as female safety and even my own safety in the area for long time, long before the hotel situation. I see, hear, and share the issues and concerns and fears people have daily. I like many residents have been left dismayed with local representation to get local issues resolved. I Listen and talk to people who all seem to be saying the same, that they believe the council is all about party politics and who gets the top jobs, it is not about them or the local area, the constant excuses of no money then we see them splash our council tax on failed follies, people feel frustrated as there is no alternative!

I have been involved with local education as a school governor and chair of governors at St Theresas Primary School along with the former St Gregorys, for over 20 years. Whilst working with some

Having spent my early years in Gildersome, I have lived in Leeds for most of my life.  I worked for the NHS and volunteer for two charities.  More recently I have been involved with transport issues in the city.

A few years ago I felt that I could not vote for either the Labour or Conservative parties and discovered the existence of and then joined the Yorkshire Party.  We want a stronger Yorkshire in a fairer United Kingdom with more decisions made in Yorkshire through a Yorkshire Regional Parliament along the lines of Scotland and Wales.  Decisions made locally are usually better decisions.  As well as devolution from Westminster to Yorkshire I support the existence of local parish and town councils and would like to see these strengthened.  

Too often London and Scotland get the lion’s share of any resources available with Yorkshire taken for granted and overlooked.  We are dependent for funds on hand-outs from Westminster government, the terms for which are dictated by London based ministers and civil servants.  England has one of the most centralised systems of government in the world. We are being poorly served by a UK government which seeks to micromanage local decisions such as cycle lanes but fails to get to grips with the big national issues we face.  

We are a centrist party which bases its policies on forward thinking and pragmatic decision-making.  Have a look at our wider policies and manifesto on our website.    

If you vote Labour things may be slightly better, they could hardly be worse than what we have had under the Conservatives, but is that the change we need?  Real devolution to Yorkshire is needed to give us better transport, health and social care, tackle crime and the housing crisis, and provide Yorkshire with a growing economy.  I am standing in this election to give you that alternative choice.

My name is Mark Dobson and I am honoured to have had the privilege of representing Garforth,  Swillington, Great & Little Preston, the Glencoe Estate and,  more recently, Skelton Lakes on Leeds City Council for the last 19 years. Before that I was a community activist and chair of the local residents’ association. I’ve lived in the community for the last 38 years.

In my day job , I am Chief Operating Officer  of a Queens Award-winning charity supporting older people. In my spare time I am a member , and three-time President, of Garforth Lions and I am currently president of both Garforth  Brass and Garforth Rec  Bowling Club.

Over the years I have been involved in many high profile campaigns, such as saving our fire station from closure (on three occasions), fighting to protect our community from an unwanted category A prison, and twice working with the community to stop Labour’s housing plans for 10,000 houses , with no supporting infrastructure, on the Greenbelt. A challenge we are soon to face again.

But it is the day-to-day involvement and engagement with other local people, dealing with thousands of pieces of individual casework, that gives me the most satisfaction. These can be issues of huge significance to the everyday work of getting somebody’s bin emptied. I give due diligence and respect to every piece of casework and attempt to answer in a timely and efficient manner.

Politics is not a precise science, and I can’t always get the desired result, but I guarantee I will always try. That I promise.

We llive in very troubling times. I understand that people want change. I understand that people are fed up with a political elite and an establishment that takes you and your votes for granted. I understand the frustration that local people feel when their voices are ignored.

I share those frustrations. For the last eight years you have voted for independents. You have been trailblazers by electing independently minded people who put our community first, no

I am proud to announce that I have been selected as the Reform UK candidate for Garforth and Swillington, including Great and Little Preston and Skelton Lakes.

I was born and raised in Leeds and am proud to call Garforth my home. Like many of you, I’ve had enough of paying more and getting less from our council. So much so that I have stood up and said enough is enough. For the first time ever I am standing as a candidate.

Council tax keeps rising — but what do we see in return? Roads riddled with potholes, community facilities closing, and high streets in decline. Meanwhile, our city is drowning in debt, paying an astonishing £1.8 million in interest every single week. Just imagine what that money could do for Swillington, or Great and Little Preston.

This Labour-run council has lost its way. It has prioritised the wrong things — spending hundreds of thousands on translation services, while pushing through large-scale developments that risk turning our area into a building site for the next 10–15 years. All of this, with no matching investment in schools, GP services, or infrastructure. Residents are being ignored. That must change.

I bring over 30 years of experience as a Project Manager, solving complex problems and managing budgets under pressure. I’ve worked in the NHS on projects with national and international impact, and in local government, where I’ve seen first-hand how waste and poor decisions hold communities back.

I don’t deal in excuses — I deliver results. My approach is simple: cut waste, challenge bad decisions, and focus on what actually matters to residents.

That means fixing our roads, protecting vital services, supporting our farmers and local businesses, tackling anti-social behaviour, and restoring pride in our communities. We need better visibility.

I will be your strong voice — standing up for residents, demanding accountability, and fighting for the investment and respect our area deserves.

Reform UK is already making an impact acro

Hi, I’m AJ, your Green Party candidate for Garforth and Swillington.

I’m a 21 year old undergraduate student at  Leeds University, studying PPE (Philosophy, Politics, and Economics). I enjoy sport, representing the university’s hockey second team, as well as playing in a 5-a-side football league.

I joined the Green Party because they’re the only party seriously challenging the status-quo and standing up to the rise of the far right. Unlike all other major parties they don’t accept donations from large organisations, and are thus free to produce policy based on integrity and morals.

In this polarised political landscape, the Greens offer hope and stability, and it truly is a privilege to be part of such a movement. Being a councillor is an opportunity for me to help improve and give back to this wonderful city which has given me so much.

I love Leeds, and it would be a true honour to work for and represent the people of Garforth and Swillington.

Salma couldn’t be more rooted in the Gipton and Harehills Ward - she was born and brought up in Harehills and lives in Gipton. Salma is an inspirational woman who has worked hard representing the Gipton and Harehills Ward on the Council. From taking up casework for local residents facing tough times through no fault of their own, to speaking up for Gipton and Harehills in the Council Chamber and on the media, Salma is a real champion for all the communities in the Gipton and Harehills Ward. Salma has been at the centre of supporting the community through covid. On top of that, on the Council she took on major responsibility during the covid crisis, playing a leading role pushing access to life-saving vaccines in Gipton and Harehills and across the whole city. Salma is a powerful voice and an energetic champion for the community she lives in and grew up in.

I lived in the constituency for several years until recently when I moved nearby. I joined Reform UK because I believe we do not have a party for hard working people or pensioners. Labour and the Conservatives have successively funded their extravagant ideas via the taxation of hard-working families whilst failing to provide any value for money for the services they receive via their taxation. I previously worked in policing and currently work in finance.

I undertook two degrees one of which is in employment law which has given me a strong understanding of workers’ rights and unlike the Conservatives and Labour I am firmly in favour of a balance between business and trade unions neither holding excessive power which as we have seen with various strikes and poor employee benefits is not beneficial.

Why vote for me?

I will challenge excessive developments which many residents are concerned with - both Labour and the Conservatives have committed to continue with these due to excessive immigration and poor planning which they have no idea how to tackle.

The area needs growth and a fair system which is why I support a raise in the income tax threshold to £20,000 to benefit all workers.

Local policing - anti social behaviour is becoming an ever-dominant issue therefore we need more accountability - the devolution of the PCC Office to the Mayor has reduced the ability to tackle this head on. After previously working in policing, I understand the need for transparency and clear goals therefore I would challenge district commanders on this directly if resolutions are not found.

Finally personal issues - I would personally deal with any issues brought to my office by constituents and set out a plan of how I will tackle any issues raised with each individual.

I am new to politics; I have no desire to be a politician for the entirety of my career but I do believe it is vital that I have some input now. Career politicians have failed to represent the public for far too lo

Hi, I am Pat
I have lived here in the heart of the ward for over 50 years.
Local politics is important and I am very concerned about what goes on around me.
We need councillors who have the time for everyone – I do I am retired. I have also been a career for many years.
We need fly tippers stopped, clean litter free environments, bins emptied, safe places for children to play, our precious nurseries being kept open/reopened after the cruel cuts by the Labour council, we need support services for the elderly, fully funded council care homes, preservation of historic buildings, no building on green belt, preservation of our cultural history and opportunities for business growth.
The council is so badly managed wasting money on this daft scheme or that. I want to see every penny of our tax money scrutinised, questions asked like do we actually need this, who will benefit and as your local Conservative councillor represent what the communities’ views are.
We should be looking at creating wealth for our city, matched funding wherever possible and encouraging new business whilst supporting the existing ones

What motivated me to become one of your Councillors?

I spent years working with new parents, seeing their lives and hopes damaged by cuts to services. I felt compelled to stand up, speak up and show up for my community and to do things differently. It took time and persistence

My first year was hard, with so much to learn. But now with Oliver and Sonia we've formed a strong, creative and effective team. We combine our strengths and skills so that we can serve you as forcefully as possible.

Most of all it's about people, about community and building trusted relationships. I cannot always do what people want, but I'll always show up and listen.

I'll challenge decisions where possible to secure better outcomes and find creative solutions to problems.

Previous Tory Councillors never had responsibility for balancing budgets slashed by 14 years of their government's cuts. Those cuts caused lasting damage and sometimes forced difficult decisions, like with parking charges.

Today we have a Government that has given Leeds more funding, and more certainty. It means we can do more: road repairs, playgrounds, tackling waste crime and more things that make life better!

For me, it's not a job, but a vocation. The hours are long and the demands are relentless, but every success makes it all worthwhile. I commit to doing all the good I can, with all the means available, for as long as you give me the opportunity to do so.

As a former business owner, with many years’ experience in the technology and services industry, I decided to stand as councillor because of something my lovely wife said to me. We were discussing the current states of politics, which gives me great cause for concern.  Especially for what kind of life is ahead for our grandchildren. She said, “Don’t just moan about it, do something about it.” So I decided to do just that.

How many times do you hear the news lead with one disastrous political decision after another? Out of control illegal immigration, national security scandals,  a welfare state that is so out of control it actually pays people not to work – decisions all made by people in key positions lacking experience and making ill-advised judgements, while all the time our streets are becoming more and more unsafe, and our economy is on the brink of collapse.

I’m standing for councillor to ‘do something about it.’ To use my business skills to put the people of Rawdon and Guiseley first in order to preserve our local area. I know what it takes to deliver results from many years’ experience running a business and I’m here to make sure that change is delivered.  I’m here to fix what’s broken, to get results for residents of ALL cultures in my constituency. Whether that’s making our area a safer place to live, fighting the housing of illegal immigrants, sorting out pot holes or bin collections -  I’m here to help make sure those changes happen.

Having lived in North West Leeds since 1992, I understand the everyday priorities of families and working people in this area.

Through over thirty years of working in local education, I have seen how closely issues such as transport, housing and family support are connected, and how important it is that local services work together.

I believe that the best decisions begin with listening. I value strong relationships with residents and community organisations, and believes these conversations are essential for shaping practical, effective change.

Yorkshire deserves its fair share. Public spending in Yorkshire was £2,600 less per person than in London or Scotland in 2023, and the trend was continued. That’s a shortfall of over £14 billion across Yorkshire

What could Yorkshire do with an extra £14 billion a year?

Avoid council tax rises

Fight crime with more frontline police - Crime is rising and we need to prioritise prevention and detection

Properly fund the essentials, such as social care and children's services.

Build a West Yorkshire Metro - Metros have elevated and underground sections to cut through congestion, making them quicker and more reliable than trams. London has Crossrail and HS2. Yorkshire deserves world-class transport to create jobs and cut pollution.

Create jobs in local health, dental and social care - Waiting hours for ambulances or in A&E is unacceptable - as are the huge shortages in dentistry, social care and children’s services

Rejuvenate derelict sites with greener homes - We need more homes to lower rents and prices. New homes should have solar panels and first-class insulation, existing homes need green subsides

I'll also fight to defend the greenbelt, which is under threat yet again.

As a teacher, I'll defend our schools - what's happened to Queensway must never happen again.

I live in Rawdon and teach engineering apprentices. 
I'll fight for you and our area's future.

Dr Bob Buxton  - your local Yorkshire Party candidate.

I'm a Mum of 4 who moved to the area 20 years ago to live with my now husband who has lived here for generations. I am passionate about inclusion and improving the lives of children who have SEND and have spent years campaigning for improvements to the system. More recently I became a governor at Queensway Primary School and St Margaret's horsforth and am a member of the Leeds schools forum.

In the past I worked in the care sector, supporting elderly and disabled people. I am really passionate about ensuring their voice is heard and the impacts of policies take those voices into account.

I have campaigned locally against parking charges at netherfield road carpark as well as supporting the campaign to keep Queensway school open.

I am Standing in this election to ensure that the:
	allocation of resources for the Harewood Ward are not compromised 
	Council consistently demonstrates value for money
	provision of services and basic standards are maintained and improved
	local infrastructure is consistently maintained to a high standard
	Leeds Local Plan does not impact negatively on the Harewood Ward
	Neighbourhood Plans remain valid
	Green Belt remains Green Belt
	Environment we share is improved
	Challenges we face are dealt with effectively and efficiently

I’m proud to have lived in Headingley and Hyde Park all my life. I know this part of the city well, and I care passionately about our local community.

Over 15 years of volunteering here – along with raising in excess of £32,000 for Local and National Charities – has shown me how we can make life even better.

Through my work with Neighbourhood Watch – which covers over 2,000 households and over 4,800 members – I understand how important it is that everybody can feel safe and protected. Like all of our public servants, the police are overworked and overstretched, so we’re working with the local community to encourage and support them.

I’ve also been working with local refuse collection teams to tackle issues of missed bin collections and persistent fly tipping, and am in regular contact with highways on issues around parking and dangerous speeding in the ward.

We have a large student population in Headingley and Hyde Park, so I also work closely with the Universities, Unipol and local landlords to help protect student tenants and ensure their homes are well maintained and that they are being treated fairly.

Being elected as a City Councillor for this ward would empower me to do so much more on all of these issues. Every vote counts, so your support is greatly appreciated!

I am a fourth generation Horsfordian and a local community leader. I was Horsforth Town Councillor for Woodside Ward between 2017-2023

I have a vast wealth of experience helping my community. I volunteer with older members of the Horsforth Community and currently Chair Dementia Friendly Horsforth.

For the past 4 years Horsforth Ward has been let down by its local Labour Councillors at a City level.
They have repeatedly voted for plans that have damaged the town like the imposition of parking charges at Fink Hill and stayed silent over our Greenbelt being under threat. I will not stand for this.

I want to see real positive changes in the area and if you vote for me in this election that is what you will see. A strong voice for Horsforth Ward.

Promoted by Neil Hunt on behalf of Chris Calvert at 126, LS18 5PX

Hiya! I’m Khadijah. I’ve lived in Horsforth since I was six, and I went to Broadgate Primary and Horsforth High.

I’m now studying Law at university, driven by my interest in racial justice. I am also an advocate for LGBTQ+ and women’s rights. I’m especially focused on counter‑terrorism powers and how wrongful stop and search unfairly targets people of colour.

These issues are a big part of why my values align so strongly with the Green Party and the vision they have for the country.

With the current cost of living crisis, so many people are struggling. Some bills have risen by 60% since 2020. I think supporting local businesses and choosing more locally grown produce can make a real difference.

I’m also passionate about groups like Horsforth Climate Action, which promote reducing, reusing and recycling — things I try to put into practice every day. I’ve recently started volunteering at Horsforth Community Kitchen, a community‑run, pay‑as‑you‑feel café that helps prevent food waste.

I genuinely believe social and economic issues are deeply connected to protecting our environment, and the Green Party offers thoughtful, practical solutions to both.

Born and brought up in Gipton I I later lived in Seacroft for over 30 years and now live in Garforth. I attended the former Parklands Girls’ High School and later worked at the school and at Leeds East Academy. In 2015 I was elected as a Labour councillor for Killingbeck and Seacroft but became disillusioned with Labour and resigned from the party, chiefly due to Labour cutting local dementia services while spending millions on vanity projects - I believe that the party has lost touch with the electorate and no longer represents its traditional core voters in places like Leeds East. I joined the SDP because I believe their sensible practical policies offer a real alternative to the failed two party political monopoly and to offer voters a choice. If elected I would be proud to represent Leeds East.

It has been the privilege of my life to serve this community that I love as your City Councillor.

The political landscape is changing fast – as we saw in Manchester last month with Hannah Spencer’s stunning victory in the Gorton and Denton by-election.

When I first ran for Leeds City Council, Hunslet and Riverside was considered a safe Labour seat. But with a lot of hard work and persistence, we were able to offer a different, more hopeful vision for Leeds.

Together, we’ve done so much – but there’s so much more to do. Many thanks in advance if you can support me to serve another 4 years.

I’m proud to be your **Labour & Co-operative** candidate for Hunslet and Riverside - as a lifelong resident, I love our communities and I know personally the challenges we face, as I face them with you.

I’m already working hard for our area: serving as a school governor at Greenmount Primary and The Ruth Gorse Academy, supporting local charities like The Leeds Community African Trust, and volunteering at the Beeston Festival, and both the Greemount and Beeston Hill Resident Associations'.

I’ve also shadowed local Cllr Paul Wray for a year. I’ll be ready to help residents from day one if you put your faith in me.

My dad, who is in the RAF, taught me the value of public service. That is exactly why I want to give back to the place where I grew up - to be a strong local voice who will make a difference every day.

Our local councillors, Paul Wray and Mohammed Iqbal, work hard to put our community first. I want to join them to build a safer, healthier area, supporting everyone, but also ensuring the voices of women and young people are back at the table locally, and heard in the halls of power at Leeds Civic Hall - as they should be.

**Beeston Hill & Hunslet Moor**

_Your Main Concerns:_
Litter, fly-tipping, drugs, housing, and jobs.

_My Commitments:_
- Cleaner Streets: Install more litter bins and ensure targeted enforcement against fly-tipping.
- Tackling Crime: Work directly with the police to address drug issues.
- Better Housing: Crack down on rogue landlords.
Local Opportunity: Bring job fairs and skills training to the area.

**Hunslet, Hunslet Carr & Green**

_Your Main Concerns:_
Fly-tipping, housing repairs, and anti-social behaviour.

_My Commitments:_
- Tackling Waste: Secure targeted enforcement against fly-tipping.
- Housing Standards: Pressure landlords to speed up essential repairs.
- Safer Communities: Invest in youth services to address the root causes of anti-social behaviour.

**Leeds City Centre & Riverside**

_Your Main Concerns:_
Youth anti-s

I feel proud to announce that I am a Nominated Candidate for REFORM UK and have an opportunity to represent and stand in the Elections in Leeds, UK on 7th May 2026.
I am a proud British Muslim with Indian origin and am an Immigrant to UK. I have always been an advocate for the frail and elderly and helped everyone to the best of my ability as a Financial Advisor and as local community helper. I have 25 years of Banking and Finance experience working with HSBC, Barclays , Santander 
My decision was based on what I have faced in past 2 years. 
I went through a very very tough time in my life ( diagnosed with Cancer and given 8 months to live but survived against odds, Domestic Abuse, Etc)  and was failed by the system and the Government. All my appeals for help and support fell on deaf ears .
As a result my Health, job, my company and my personal life suffered with major consequences. 
This is when , instead of breaking Down completely,  I decided to do something about it. 
I want to make sure what I went through doesn't happen to others. I want to make a change for the better , improve and speed the system . Make our land , space and community a better place to live and enjoy with dignity . Make UK great again.
Proud moment for  my Parents and Family in India too. I speak Bengali, Hindi, Urdu and English fluently. 
I have been nominated for awards for community. I run an Elderly CIC called D Dance Theatre CIC which helps people living with long term illness and the elderly to recover and cope with Bollywood Dancing

Make Leeds an easy city for drivers, cyclists and pedestrians to navigate. Many of the council’s projects seem to punish drivers and set different road users against each other, justified on achieving net zero targets. Spending money on good road maintenance works better for all residents.
 
We believe in a more equal society, free from prejudice. As such, we reject the current obsession with identity politics which divides society into hostile and opposing camps. By strengthening the common bonds between us we can rebuild a sense of community.
 
Councils need to uphold the provision of single sex spaces and services based on biological sex to provide women and girls with safety and dignity, and to protect the council from legal claims that may be brought now that the law is clear.
 
Make the council accountable to Leeds residents all the time not just at election time by creating more opportunities to hear what residents actually think in person, rather than through consultations and focus groups often outsourced to outside organisations with their own agendas.
 
Defend freedom of speech and thought as core British values.

Born and lived in Leeds most of my life and my family have been in Leeds since the 1890’s.

Work & Education

Solicitor and Photographer, MA in International Studies specialising in the Middle East, Islamic Radicalism and the Cold War, Leeds University. LLM in Commercial Intellectual Property Law, Nottingham Trent

Why Reform UK

I have been active in local politics for over 30 years with both Labour and the Liberal Democrats. I have concluded that only a shift to Reform will save the country from failed Tory and Labour policies.

What Leeds needs

I have been advocating a mass transit system for Leeds based on a Monorail that will catapult the profile of the city worldwide also we need an upgrade to the Airport without delay for the future prosperity of the city.

Hello! My name is Nosheen and I’m the Green Party candidate for Killingbeck and Seacroft in the May 2026 elections.

I’ve lived in Seacroft for five years and work in the transport industry as a Controller.

I’ve always enjoyed living in Seacroft due to the numerous community intitiatives in the area, which I try to participate in as much as I’m able to.

I enjoy the massive community spirit that exists in the area and great warmth shown by many local residents to each other and towards me, making it a great pleasure to live here!

Born and brought up in Leeds, Rosemary attended Lawnswood High School before going on o study at the universities of Bristol and Durham. On completing her teacher training she returned to Leeds to take up her first teaching post in Meanwood. After taking time out from teaching to bring up her three children, she retrained as a dyslexia specialist and has spent the last 21 years helping people of all ages overcome the literacy and numeracy difficulties associated with dyslexia and other forms of neurodiversity.

Her vision for Seacroft is for it to be a safe, welcoming community where everyone can lead their best life possible. She is particularly interested in housing, tackling antisocial behaviour and the management of open spaces."

Rosemary lives in Seacroft with her husband Peter and their cocker spaniel.

Rosemary can be contacted via email eastleedstories@gmail.com and is happy to answer any questions you may have.

It’s time again for us in Kippax, Methley, Allerton Bywater, Micklefield, Ledston, Ledsham and Ledston Luck choose who we want to represent us on the council.

I’ve seen a bit in the past few weeks from other parties, so I wanted to put something out there to explain why I’m standing for re-election to represent my home.

Some of you might remember me from my time in The Moorgate. In fact, it’s one of the things I love the most about representing the village - keeping in touch with those familiar old faces who I used to serve from behind the bar.

Now I serve Kippax from the Leeds City Council chamber - it’s my full time job and one I’m absolutely committed to.

I’ve seen some of the promises banded about by unknown candidates from other parties, and whilst our area normally votes Labour, I know that this time might be incredibly close.

That’s why I’m appealing to everyone to take a step back and look at the choice in front of you. I know there’s a lot of unhappiness about national politics, and I know some people just want to give the Labour Party a bad day, but I work everyday for our community and have done for decades. I hold regular advice sessions, have done my best to protect our villages from massive Tory cuts and am incredibly committed to this community.

Or there’s another choice. We can vote for the same chaos that people have seen where unknown Reform candidates took power last year - promises to cut council tax ripped to shreds, constant internal bickering, their Councillors focused on things decided in Westminster rather than delivering for their community.

So if and when you go to vote on 7 May, remember these important bits:

- ⁠It’s not a national election, we’re electing a local councillor to represent our community on the council.
- ⁠I’ve spent forty years serving my community and will continue to do so if you want me to.
- You need to take photo ID to vote.
- ⁠Those who shout the loudest and make the most promises aren’t always the ones you’re

I live in Kippax and I'm currently a councillor on Kippax Parish Council, so I know how local government works but I'm not a career politician, and I never want to be. I'm just someone who loves this area, who's been out on the doorsteps speaking with people for the last year and who has heard the same thing again and again: we feel forgotten and we need change.

The Conservatives ran this country for 14 straight years, and Labour have run Kippax and Methley for the last few decades unopposed. They are not doing the job they once did. The potholes, our green spaces under threat, the fact that they've taken Leeds City Council into £2.6 billion of debt - these aren't small things. They're impacting our daily life here, they are getting worse, and they've been neglected for too long by people who assumed your vote was already in the bag.

We're the only people with a proper plan to fix Leeds City Council and protect Kippax + Methley. I bring a decade of professional experience outside of politics, policies, fresh eyes, and the energy to turn things around and bring some hope back.

On Thursday 7th May, I'm asking for your vote. Not out of habit, not because that's just the way we've always voted, but because you genuinely deserve something different. You deserve someone that can get the potholes fixed, stop the overdevelopment in our villages, get council tax down, and fix the mess that we're in.

Vote Chris Weightman for Reform UK on Thursday 7th May. We're counting on you to help us bring change to Kippax and Methley.

I have been living in Micklefield with my wife and daughter for five years and love the area. I also have a close connection with the area as my family history can be traced back to this village, so I have always felt this is my home.

Like many others, I joined the Green party after becoming disillusioned by the state of the Labour Party. I feel they no longer represent everyday people, social issues or day to day living.

I’m standing for my area as a Green Party candidate because I believe we can do better. Better as a community, and better at looking out for one another.

I want to be the person who is willing to listen and then listen some more to truly understand the people’s needs.

As your Green Party candidate for Kirkstall, I have big ambitions for improving our neighbourhoods.

We are of course lucky to live in a community with so many green areas, but I believe there is a lot that could be done to make those areas even better:

* Improving accessibility so that everybody can enjoy our green spaces
* Ensuring local nature spots are well-connected and abundant with wildlife
* Investing in new riverside footpaths and footbridges to make local green spaces safer and more walkable
* Finding ways to better manage of the resources we already have.

As a local party, we have been organising litter picks to keep our neighbourhoods clean and safe for everyone, and we have even bigger clean-ups planned for the future.

I also believe there is much that can be done to make our community safer and more pedestrian friendly.

My plans for this include:

* Proper pedestrian crossings at the intersection of Wyther Lane and Broad Lane
* St Ann’s Lane becoming one-way at the Burley Road end
* Introducing traffic calming measures around Kirkstall Valley Primary School.

Labour have taken our area for granted for too long. We shouldn’t be expected to make do with second best. Our community deserves higher standards.

Together we can make Kirkstall a greener, healthier and more connected place to live.

Hannah moved to Leeds over a decade ago when she came to university. She currently works as a geography teacher in Bradford and has previously worked on the railways.
She is involved in many facets of the community: guides, inner west community committee, and kirkstall neighbourhood forum.
As a teacher and guide leader she has a passion for youth issues and has been at the front line of public sector cuts and seeing the damage that the Tory austerity is having on the education system and the wider lives of people.

Leeds is my home, and I’ve seen first-hand how national and local politicians have sought to divide our communities and ignore the problems that residents face every day.

Whether it’s the lack of affordable housing, inaccessible public transport, our strained public services, or the upkeep of our green spaces, I promise to keep fighting to improve the lives of residents across Little London and Woodhouse.

By working with local housing groups, I’ve seen how easy it is for homeless people to fall through the gaps in council processes. With tenant unions, I’ve seen how an unfair system worsens and perpetuates housing insecurity. With local transport campaign groups, I’ve protested against stripping back services that would leave our disabled residents without a reliable way to get around the city.

It’s these community voices that need a seat at the table; there should be nothing decided about us, without us.

Politicians can and should be working to create a fairer system, but so often they aren’t. I’m standing to be your Green councillor in Little London and Woodhouse to make sure that happens.

My name is James Kendall, and I am the Reform UK candidate for Middleton Park Ward. Over the past two years, I have worked hard to build our grassroots branch from the ground up, focusing on engaging with local residents and strengthening our presence in the community.

In my professional life, I am a manager with extensive leadership experience. Previously, I served as a regional manager for the largest privately owned courier company in the UK, where I was responsible for overseeing operations, managing teams, and delivering results in a fast-paced environment.

After the last general election, where I stood in Leeds South West and Morley, I made a promise that I would come home and represent my area — and it would be a privilege to do so.

Leeds is an amazing city, and the residents of Middleton and Belle Isle are just as remarkable. We are hardworking and community-focused people. However, in recent years, that sense of community has begun to fade. Local pubs have closed, we have lost our sports centre, and small businesses are struggling, often overshadowed by larger shopping centres.

Hi. My name is Alexandria Head, your Green Party candidate for Middleton Park Ward.

I joined the Green Party because I feel Labour has been a huge disappointment. I used to support Labour, mainly when Jeremy Corbyn was the Party Leader. But when Zack Polanski became the leader of the Green Party, I felt there was potential again.

The Green Party not only aims to introduce environmental justice but also social justice. This is what I want from any party, but the Green Party is one of the few UK parties that wants to implement these justices.

I am standing in the local elections for Middleton Park ward because I want to ensure that environmental justice is achieved at the council level. I want to make sure that Middleton Park is safe for everyone and that the streets are cleaner.

Rachel has been the Moortown ward candidate since 2019, came 2nd place in the last local election in 2024 and is looking forward to standing again on May 7th 2026.

Rachel set up the successful campaign group Healthy Air Leeds, and fundraised for the new play area as a committee member of the Meanwood Park Playground Appeal.

Rachel is a long standing member of the local Litter Free Meanwood group and is a big supporter of the wonderful independent businesses we have in Meanwood and Moortown.

“I am passionate about keeping Meanwood and Moortown clean and green, with safe, accessible transport and active travel routes for all.”

Sharon Slinger is the Liberal Democrat candidate for Moortown, known locally for getting stuck in and working alongside residents to make things happen.

She lives in Meanwood with her family and is an active part of the local community. Sharon runs her own small business and is a Fellow of the Royal Institution of Chartered Surveyors, bringing a strong sense of professionalism and accountability to everything she does.

Sharon has been closely involved in local campaigns, including pushing for action on traffic issues at Meanwood junction and supporting residents in successfully challenging proposals that would have negatively impacted the area. She is also a visible presence locally - reporting and following up on graffiti, raising everyday concerns, and making sure residents’ voices are heard.

Rather than just raising issues, Sharon focuses on getting results. She works with residents, asks the right questions, and keeps pressure on decision-makers until action is taken. Her approach is practical and persistent - not just promises, but follow-through.

For Moortown, Sharon would bring:

* A strong track record of listening to residents and acting on what matters to them
* A hands-on approach to local issues, from traffic and road safety to environmental concerns
* Determination to see things through and get outcomes, not just responses
* A commitment to being visible, accessible, and accountable as a local councillor

Sharon is standing to ensure Moortown has a councillor who works hard, stays engaged with the community, and focuses on delivering real improvements for local people.

Terry is well known around Morley and is a Town Councillor, a former Mayor of Morley, chairman of the Morley Remembrance and Fair trade Committees as well as a committed fundraiser to help Ukraine.

Terry is a former business owner in Morley, a former City Councillor and helped set up Morley Elderly Action.

Terry has lived in Morley all his life, went to school in Morley and works in Morley.

His experience within and commitment to the Town and surrounding areas can be seen in the work he has undertaken across Morley over many years.

On Thursday 12 June please use your vote in the Morley South By-election to vote for Terry Grayshon - Morley Borough Independents.
I'm standing as the Labour Party candidate in Morley North to be a strong voice for the area on the Labour Group on Leeds City Council.

Hello, my name is Amber, your Green Party candidate for Morley North.

Since moving to Leeds in 2024 for university, I now consider Leeds my home and have a deep love for the city.

I joined the Green Party in 2023 because it was the only major political party that recognised the links between environmental and social justice. Through volunteering with the party, I have been involved with a range of campaigning and activism, from litter picking and door knocking to attending marches advocating for women’s rights, standing against racism and in solidarity with Palestine. I have also been working as treasurer for the Leeds University Green Party Society, helping to create a community of inspired young activists who further support the amazing work being done by the party.

I am standing to be the candidate for Morley North as I want to ensure that local concerns such as community safety, affordable housing and improving public services are addressed. The Green Party’s national policies are inherently aligned with supporting local communities and businesses, and I would ensure that local interests are central to my work as a councillor.

Hi, I’m James Trueman.
My family has been part of the Morley community for the past 20 years, with my children attending schools in Gildersome and then Morley. I spent six years coaching with Gildersome Spurs Juniors, which gave me the chance to support opportunities and a team environment for local young people..
Professionally, I’ve worked as Head of Finance for manufacturing companies in Leeds, helping to support jobs, investment and growth in our area.

Michael is a local Morley resident and works in Leeds. He was previously a commissioned officer in the Royal Navy and served in vessels stationed in the Middle East. He joined the Liberal Democrats after the 2017 general election, campaigning for a fairer and more liberal Britain.

In this election, Michael is standing to provide a real alternative for Morley. There isa  clear choice in this election between Reform and Lib Dem Michael Fox. Support for the Lib Dems surged in last year's local by-election while every other party lost support. Only Michael Fox and the Lib Dems are building the support needed ti sop Reform this time.

Michael is committed to working for Morley and dealing with the issues that matter to people here. Residents have been saying they want action to fix pot holes, improve bus services and make our streets clean and tidy again. Working with Liberal Democrat colleagues on the council Michael would push for additional PCSOs to help deal with crime and anti-social behaviour on Queen Street.

By voting for Michael and the Liberal Democrats you would be sending a message to those who have let us down and also getting a strong local voice on the council at the same time.

I love this area, but as in many other parts of Leeds, walking around you can clearly see the signs of how inequality is taking a toll on the community.

My vision for Leeds is a city where everyone can access affordable housing that is safe to live in, where public transport is cheap and reliable, and where people aren’t struggling just to survive.

I joined the Green Party because they are the only party with real ambition to create a fairer society. We must expand social housing and bring transport into public ownership. How can it be fair that the basic things we all need in life are monopolised by multi-nationals and rented back to us

I reject completely the divisive politics that tells us migration is the reason people are struggling. Together we can transform Leeds into a City of Belonging.

Hello, I’m Adrian Blake, and I’m standing as the candidate for Morley South on behalf of Labour and Co-operative.

Morley is such an incredibly special and unique town, and I’m so lucky to be raising my family amongst the wonderful Morley community. There's much to be proud of here, but there's also a lot we can do to improve things.

I’m not the type of candidate to promise easy solutions, or to claim I can do things outside the remit of a councillor. I know the strain and restraints that councils are operating under. Our councillors are not sat in front of a big “More funding” or “Fix all the potholes” button, that they are too lazy to press. The hardest duty of a councillor is to decide the best use of limited resource. That said, the reason I’m running is that there are changes I can make. As your Labour and Co-operative councillor, I’d be working closely with the Leeds Labour Council to guide and drive real changes we need here in Morley.

🔴 I’d resist the short-term minded sales of our public assets, so that future generations can enjoy them.

🔴 I’d encourage and maximise Morley’s access to the Local Power Plan, to get grants and funding for council, or local co-operative owned solar power on council or community owned buildings to benefit our community.

🔴 I’d cooperate with the local police to see what help they need from the council to best tackle, deter and dampen the effects of anti-social behaviour.

🔴 I'd push to make sure that people start to actually see their council tax in action. That means potholes, that means bin collections. Most council funding is mandated for spend on vital but often invisible services such as social care, whilst visible services have degraded. So, whilst I’ll obviously be pushing for our fair share of any funding, this is going to mainly come down to chasing council services for constituents, and pushing for new, more efficient ways to do things than we already are.

I work as an optimisation consultant, primarily for waste m

The Green Party candidate for Leeds NW constituency in the forthcoming General Election is Mick Bradley. Mick has lived in the constituency for 12 years with his wife and teenage son.  He worked for Citizens Advice Bureau including setting up a new centre in Balham, South London dealing with housing, welfare benefits and debt. Then was a primary teacher and later trained teachers in global education including human rights, sustainable development and global citizenship. He most enjoys being in nature and has completed all the 282 Munro hills in Scotland and Wainwright's in the Lake District. Mick has been an Otley Town Councillor for over 9 years. As an active councillor, he has been fully engaged with making Otley a better place to live by getting a flood study carried out after the serious 2015 flooding leading to the present flood alleviation scheme. Mick started the annual Family Cycling Day which allows young children to cycle an off road course away from traffic and having secured funding is leading work in particular, with local schools, to encourage active travel around town. He is a founder member and trustee of Otley 2030, which seeks a carbon neutral and fair town in the 2030's.

Hi, I’m Mikey Sykes, your Green Party candidate for Rothwell.

I grew up in Woodlesford and still live here, so I know this area well and hold it very close to my heart. I’m standing because I want to be a strong representative for people in Rothwell, Carlton, Oulton and Woodlesford, particularly those who are struggling with the cost of living and feeling left behind by the Labour government.

I joined the Green Party in 2024 after being a Labour voter for many years. Like many others, I felt I could no longer support the Labour Party after they abandoned their pledges. I joined the Green Party because they are the only mainstream party focused on fighting hatred and discrimination, standing up for working people against big business and protecting the most vulnerable in our society.

As your councillor for Rothwell, I would be focused on local issues that really matter to this community. Rent prices have become far too high and I know many people who are being priced out of the area. Poor phone signal is also a major issue for many local residents, particularly those living in the Oulton area.

Too often areas like ours are overlooked and taken for granted. I will be a loud and proud representative for Rothwell on Leeds City Council.

I grew up here – I love our community and our city.

I envisage a Roundhay where everyone can access thriving local services, public transport is affordable and reliable, our environment and green spaces are protected, and people have a real choice over housing – all within a more compassionate, cohesive Leeds.

This is the vision I will fight for, if elected on 7 May – are you with me?

I was born in Hong Kong to British parents I grew up in Harrogate and I have lived in Leeds since I started in a management position within the Supermarket industry. I started work at 15, full time from 18, working hard to progress. I now work in Motorsport, one of my loves.

This country needs change but not through high tax and following the disastrous decline we have seen over the last decades. Reform UK are the right party to make a real change.

JOIN REFORM UK TODAY

Let's Make Britain Great We can make Britain great again.

To do this, reform is essential in the way our country is run and managed, so it works properly for the people.

In many areas, just the application of basic common sense would be a good start! The nation faces many challenges, but we can overcome them. To succeed, we need to do Brexit properly. We must grow our way out of the crisis, we cannot tax our way out of it. We must stand up for our core democratic values, our civil liberties, our right to free speech.

Let’s celebrate our pride in being British: our amazing culture, our unbreakable communities, our incredible heritage. Let’s stop all the woke nonsense that is holding us back. Let’s have a proper immigration policy that works for our country and protects our borders. Together, let’s make great things happen!

Reform is essential in these key areas: 1. Reform our Economy:

To succeed faster growth is vital.

Higher growth rates are the only way to better wages and more tax revenues that can be invested in better healthcare and other public services.

Our bold economic vision frees up over 6 million people from paying Income Tax and frees up over 1.2 million small businesses and self-employed from paying Corporation Tax.

We would also aim to remove a raft of other stifling taxes in a responsible, timely way.

This will generate much faster growth than seen in recent decades.

We must also stop wasting taxpayers money... Read more
