Thank you for taking the time to read my profile.
I am your local candidate as I live in the heart of Temple Newsam ward.
Our mission as the LOCAL CONSERVATIVES is to protect, enhance and preserve the area we live in.
1) Stop the illegal traveller camps
2) End Labours unfair parking charges
3) Protect our area, parks and green belt
4) Fight crime and nuisance bikes
5) Preserve our history
6) Keep the nurseries open
7) Be the voice for Temple Newsam ward, rather than just going along with whatever the council says and really standing up for our area in the meetings, so we are not the forgotten bit of Leeds
8) Really scrutinise the new policies and decisions to make sure it is our ward that benefits
9) Look after our local environment by making sure bins are emptied, litter is collected and the streets are clean. 
10) Road safety must be addressed especially around school drop off times, festivals, road closures and pavements fit for purpose.
With warmest of regards
Maggie

Hi, I am Max! I am Leeds born and bred and I am passionate about my local community. I strive to improve the place that I grew up in and want to ensure that all residents of East Leeds can feel proud of their area.

In 2023, I was at the protest against the sale of our historic Crossgates library building, and it reminded me of the time Labour wanted to sell Temple Newsam Golf club. Our Labour councillors voted against an independent amendment to save the library that year. This is quite the contrast with their earlier photo opportunities feigning disappointment at the sale which was planned by their own administration. We must find a permanent home for the library, as it is currently housed in the shopping centre and taxpayers money is being wasted on rent. As a councillor I would recognise the value of our community spaces and push to repurpose them for present-day needs.

Should you have any questions, I would be delighted to hear from you, and you can contact me at: eastleedstories@gmail.com

Hi. My name is Josh Alston, your Green Party candidate for Temple Newsam.

I am a historian, researcher and activist and I am standing because I believe that Leeds can be different.

We don’t need to accept everything getting gradually worse.

In housing, care, education and transport, we’ve seen our most important institutions fall into disrepair. We can all think of a system that used to work well in the past, but now barely works at all.

The main parties can’t fix this.

If elected my focus will be on restoring local democracy. This means a bottom-up approach to all areas of policy making.

I will do more to protect our tenants from exploitative landlords, who ask extortionate rates for poor quality housing.

I will celebrate our city’s diversity, organising and supporting community projects to make our community stronger and more united.

I am delighted to be the Labour Candidate for my home ward of Weetwood. I am excited to work alongside Cllr Emma Flint who has set such a high standard and is bring about real change and our MP Alex Sobel who is delivering for our community.  I work for a local debt advice charity on the frontline of the cost of living. You'll always be able to reach me and you will see me around Weetwood, whether it's on a community walk, collecting donations for the food bank, or supporting a community project.

Last year your vote made a difference Labour Councillor Emma Flint. We're both local residents and care about the area.

Chris is a qualified accountant and now he works training accountants.

He lives in LS16 and was born and raised in North Leeds.

He has qualified as a pilot and Rugby League referee. In his spare time, he plays classical guitar and is an active member of a local church. He also sat on the board of Leeds Playhouse.

He was our area’s councillor between 2019 and 2023. He fought off the original Lawnswood Roundabout plan which would have seen it turned into an Amercan-style intersection.

He helped to secure funding for new play areas, defibrillators and to help local groups and organisations.

Chris' Priorities Chris has worked in the Leeds Central and Headingley community for years and is prioritising working on...

Helping people When Chris was a councillor, he helped huge numbers of people with their problems.

Stop the waste Labour have wasted nearly £25 million on a local road scheme that will increase congestion.

Better public transport Chris wants this money spent on more bus services.

I am shocked, appalled, and quite frankly, scared, at the accelerating inequality and environmental breakdown unfolding before our eyes. So I feel that I must do something positive to support our community and our planet. That’s why I am standing to become a Councillor in Weetwood.

As your Councillor I will work to ensure that:

* the wealthiest pay their fair share
* our NHS and schools are protected
* we are kind to immigrants, instead of demonising them 
* the natural world is prioritised
* the most vulnerable in society are looked after, and
* we put an end to politics-as-usual that is doing nothing to stop our declining living standards.

The Greens, and I, are here to provide a new, hopeful, kind politics that’s focused on solving our problems collectively.

If I become the next Weetwood Councillor I will stick up for my neighbours, and I will stand up to corporate interests.

I was born in Leeds and spent my entire childhood living in Headingley and going to local schools, so I’m not some fake Westminster import here to advance their career, I know and love Leeds.

I’ve worked for leading law firms, and in medicine and psychology research. I have many years of experience in setting up and directing successful wellbeing and sporting initiatives.

I’ll be here for the whole constituency, I will make sure that the people of Tinshill, Ireland Wood, Hawksworth, Kirkstall, Burley and Woodhouse have a champion in parliament. If you vote for me to be your representative, you will see me regularly, I won’t become an invisible man.

The Headingley I grew up in had less vomit on the street. I’d take action to reduce antisocial behaviour and bring back self-respect. I want the whole constituency to be a vibrant and joyful place to live and start a family.

Unlike Labour, Reform & the others I, along with the Social Democratic Party, will not betray the wishes of the people.

My priorities for the constituency would be:

1. Reducing housing and energy costs so your children can have hope for the future and the elderly can have the dignity they deserve.

2. Protecting Green Spaces for both nature and the enjoyment of the public. I walk in Meanwood Valley every day and I know how much it adds to our quality of life.

3. Improving public transport while keeping costs as low as possible.

As a long time resident of Wetherby, my ties to the community are deep rooted.After retiring from my career in the Prison Service as Governor of Wealstun prison, I have continued a path of service for residents, for 13 years as a Town Councillor and the last 8 years as your elected Wetherby Ward Councillor.

I believe that our area is best served by experienced, sensible representation-not uncertainty or extremes so I am asking for your support.

I grew up here and I want to make sure that Wetherby and our villages remain a place where people can build their lives, whether that’s getting around, finding a home, or feeling part of the community.

Having a young family, I know how important everyday life is. We need better transport, better access to local services and healthcare – and development that works for our community, with the right infrastructure in place.

I will be a strong local voice on practical improvements for Wetherby and our villages.

I am a proud dad, husband, dog owner and green energy lawyer. For the last three years I have served Wetherby as a Town Councillor. I’ve helped protect the X98 & X99 bus services when threatened with closure, supported bus franchising, delivered weekend markets and new tree-planting schemes in Wetherby, helped Wetherby Foodbank and consistently voted against the Tory-led town council’s precept (tax) rises to protect residents’ pockets.

My professional career is rooted in decarbonisation and tackling climate change. I’m proudly from a working class background, and was brought up to value our NHS and care for underprivileged people - those suffering from inequality - in my community.

I firmly believe in public service and social justice. If I am privileged to become Wetherby’s Ward Councillor, those are the principles I will always follow.
